Registry / Terms / accuracy class

accuracy class

VIMOutdated — VIM 1993 G 18 #00825 · 12 instances

Authoritative definition

VIM 1993 · concept #5.19view ↗

class of measuring instruments that meet certain metrological requirements that are intended to keep errors within specified limits

Outdated: Cites VIM 1993. Latest: VIM 2012.
  • VIM 1993 · #5.19
    class of measuring instruments that meet certain metrological requirements that are intended to keep errors within specified limits

Suggested actions

  1. INFO ✓ This term IS in VIM 2012 (concept #4.25). View VIM 2012 entry
  2. MEDIUM 8 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

8 distinct definitions across 12 publications. 4 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

2 identical2010202X
class designation of a weight or weight set which meets certain metrological requirements intended to maintain the mass values within specified limits
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency

class of liquid-in-glass thermometers that meet certain metrological requirements intended to keep errors within specified limits

2 identical2010202X
class of measuring instrument that meets certain metrological requirements that are intended to keep errors within specified limits

class of pressure balances that meet certain metrological requirements intended to keep errors within specified limits

Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Definition
20102000OIML R 60:20002.2.100708
class of load cells that are subject to the same conditions of accuracy [Adapted from VIM:1993, 5.19]
pending

class of measuring instrument that meets certain metrological requirements that are intended to maintain errors within specified limits
pending
202X2012OIML R046-1:20122.2.2802327-R046-1
class of measuring instruments or measuring systems that meet stated metrological requirements that are intended to keep measurement errors or instrumental measurement uncertainties within specified limits under specified operating conditions [OIML V2-200:2012, 4.25]
pending
202X2012OIML R137:20123.2.902665
class of measuring instruments or measuring systems that meet stated metrological requirements that are intended to keep measurement errors or instrumental uncertainties within specified limits under specified operating conditions [VIM, 4.25]
